top_categories.workout_context: 88ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
8,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Arlberg Giro's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
At Arlberg Giro, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Arlberg Giro's paved alpine mountain roads through the Austrian forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Arlberg Giro. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
8,200ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 93 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Arlberg Giro, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 8,200ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved alpine mountain roads through the Austrian. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Arlberg Giro’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Arlberg Giro. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Arlberg Giro requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved alpine mountain roads through the austrian alps to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, VO2max, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 93 miles of paved alpine mountain roads through the austrian alps in St. Anton am Arlberg, Tyrol, Austria.
